title: Managed audit manual sandbox connection credential resolver no-network safety fixture contract intake

noNetworkSafetyFixtureContract.sourceSpan: Node v322 closure review + Node v313 catalog
noNetworkSafetyFixtureContract.purpose: Define the no-network safety fixture shape required before any later resolver can discuss a runtime path that must refuse HTTP/TCP before approval.

requiredFields.label: Fixture id
requiredFields.purpose: Identify the no-network safety fixture without running it.

requiredFields.label: Operator confirmation handle
requiredFields.acceptedShape: operator confirmation handle, no credential value
requiredFields.purpose: Bind fixture review to a human operator confirmation.

requiredFields.label: Approval correlation id
requiredFields.purpose: Bind the fixture contract to the approval chain.

requiredFields.label: Transport denial policy id
requiredFields.purpose: Declare which no-network denial policy is expected.

requiredFields.label: Expected denied transport classes
requiredFields.acceptedShape: HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, TLS, DNS, or equivalent class names
requiredFields.purpose: List transport classes the future path must refuse before approval.

requiredFields.label: Required denial evidence
requiredFields.acceptedShape: machine-readable denial evidence handle or digest
requiredFields.purpose: Declare the evidence a later verification must observe.

requiredFields.label: Forbidden network actions
requiredFields.purpose: List network actions that must not occur in contract intake.

requiredFields.label: Cleanup marker
requiredFields.purpose: Prove any future fixture run has a cleanup requirement.

requiredFields.label: Timeout budget ms
requiredFields.purpose: Bound any future denial verification without running it in v323.

requiredFields.label: Audit digest
requiredFields.acceptedShape: sha256 digest or equivalent stable digest
requiredFields.purpose: Prove contract immutability without embedding secret or endpoint material.

prohibitedFields.reason: Credential values must not enter the no-network safety fixture contract.
Raw endpoint URLs must not enter this contract; only handles and denial policy ids are allowed.
Provider configuration would turn this contract into implementation.
Resolver client configuration is not allowed in a contract-only intake.
No HTTP/TCP payload may be prepared or sent by v323.
Opening sockets is prohibited during contract intake.
HTTP request execution is prohibited during contract intake.
TCP connection attempts are prohibited during contract intake.
Approval ledger writes remain outside this Node contract.
Schema migration SQL is prohibited in this intake.
Starting Java, mini-kv, or external audit services is prohibited.

rejectionReasons.message: The no-network safety fixture id is missing.
The transport denial policy id or expected denied transport classes are missing.
Network execution, socket opening, HTTP requests, or TCP connection attempts are prohibited.
Credential values and raw endpoint URLs are not allowed in the fixture contract.
Secret provider and resolver client config are prohibited in this intake.
Ledger writes, schema migration, deployment, rollback, upstream start, and runtime shell invocation are prohibited.

noGoBoundaries.message: v323 must not read managed audit credential values.
v323 must not parse or render raw endpoint URLs.
v323 must not instantiate secret providers.
v323 must not instantiate resolver clients.
v323 must not send HTTP/HTTPS requests.
v323 must not open TCP/TLS sockets.
v323 must not execute a no-network fixture; it only defines the contract.
v323 must not write approval ledger or schema state.
v323 must not automatically start Java, mini-kv, or external audit services.
v323 must not invoke a runtime shell.

upstreamEchoRequests.requestedEcho: Read-only echo of the Node v323 no-network safety fixture contract, confirming Java will not execute SQL, deployment, rollback, ledger writes, or external network calls.
Non-participation receipt proving mini-kv does not execute LOAD/COMPACT/RESTORE/SETNXEX, open network sockets, or become network safety authority.

necessityProof.blockerResolved: v322 completed the endpoint-handle-allowlist-approval prerequisite and named no-network-safety-fixture as the next concrete missing contract.
necessityProof.consumer: Java v149 + mini-kv v141, then Node v324
necessityProof.whyV322CannotBeReused: v322 is a closure review only; it proves endpoint-handle-allowlist-approval is complete but does not define no-network denial evidence for upstream echo.
necessityProof.existingReportReuseDecision: Reuse v322 as source state and v313 as the prerequisite catalog; create v323 only for the no-network safety fixture contract intake.
necessityProof.stopCondition: Stop if the contract requires credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client configuration, network execution, runtime shell implementation or invocation, ledger/schema writes, mini-kv authority, or automatic upstream start.

warnings.message: v323 defines the no-network fixture contract only; it does not run a fixture, open sockets, or prove denial behavior yet.
No-network safety fixture work does not close abort/rollback semantics, which remains a separate prerequisite.

recommendations.message: After v323 is archived, Java v149 and mini-kv v141 can run in parallel as read-only echo and non-participation receipt work.

Keep the no-network safety fixture as handles, denied transport classes, evidence requirements, cleanup markers, timeout budget, and digest only; never add credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client config, socket operations, or HTTP/TCP payloads.

nextActions: Archive Node v323 as the no-network safety fixture contract-only intake.
After v323 is complete, Java v149 and mini-kv v141 can run in parallel as read-only echo and non-participation receipt work.
Node v324 must wait for both Java v149 and mini-kv v141 before verifying no-network safety fixture echo alignment.

Stop immediately if the fixture contract tries to execute HTTP/TCP, instantiate providers or clients, parse raw endpoint URLs, read credentials, write ledgers/schema, invoke runtime shell behavior, or automatically start upstreams.
